responsibilities: Design and layout multi-layer printed circuit boards using Altium Designer or Cadence Allegro for consumer and industrial electronics products.
Create and maintain component footprints, schematic symbols, and PCB libraries adhering to IPC standards and manufacturing guidelines.
Perform design rule checks (DRC) and design for manufacturing (DFM) reviews to ensure fabrication and assembly readiness.
Route high-speed signals, differential pairs, and power planes while maintaining controlled impedance and minimizing crosstalk.
Collaborate with electronics and mechanical engineers to define board outline, stackup, and component placement constraints.
Generate Gerber files, drill files, and fabrication drawings for handoff to PCB manufacturers and contract assemblers.
Review and resolve signal integrity, thermal, and EMI concerns identified during board-level simulation and post-layout analysis.
Coordinate with PCB fabricators and assembly houses to resolve manufacturing queries and reduce production turnaround time.
Maintain version-controlled PCB design files and documentation throughout iterative product development cycles.
Support bring-up and debugging of prototype boards, identifying and correcting layout-related issues found during testing.
